target.title: fix(whatsapp): align message hook config
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #86390 by accepting documented WhatsApp channel/account `pluginHooks.messageReceived` config in the strict channel schema.
- Makes the WhatsApp runtime also honor the manifest-backed plugin config path `plugins.entries.whatsapp.config.pluginHooks.messageReceived`.
- Documents precedence and adds regression coverage for schema validation and hook emission.

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts src/config/config.allowlist-requires-allowfrom.test.ts`
- `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... validateConfigObject({ channels: { whatsapp: { pluginHooks: { messageReceived: true }, accounts: { work: { pluginHooks: { messageReceived: true } } } } } }) ... EOF`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-whatsapp.ts src/config/config.allowlist-requires-allowfrom.test.ts`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/channels/whatsapp.md`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp inbound `message_received` hook opt-in config now has a schema-valid documented channel/account path, and the runtime also honors the plugin manifest config path.
Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout on commit 622324d64c, Node via repo toolchain.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx - <<'EOF'` with `validateConfigObject({ channels: { whatsapp: { pluginHooks: { messageReceived: true }, accounts: { work: { pluginHooks: { messageReceived: true } } } } } })`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts src/config/config.allowlist-requires-allowfrom.test.ts`.
Evidence after fix: The config validation command printed `{ "ok": true, "errors": [] }`; the targeted Vitest command exited 0 and covers plugin-entry hook opt-in plus account-level precedence.
Observed result after fix: Documented `channels.whatsapp.pluginHooks.messageReceived` and account-scoped `channels.whatsapp.accounts.<id>.pluginHooks.messageReceived` are accepted by config validation, while runtime hook emission also works from `plugins.entries.whatsapp.config.pluginHooks.messageReceived`.
What was not tested: No live WhatsApp/Baileys connection was used; proof uses the local config validator and the WhatsApp processMessage runtime test harness with mocked transport/hook runner.
